settings.title: NMJ Lovers Revisited | Rachel and Nic
settings.description:
NMJ Lovers Revisited is a journal series exploring what happens after the proposal, the ceremony, the “I do.” While engagement and ceremonial pieces honour a moment in time, their true purpose is to remain and worn through the many seasons that follow.

This chapter revisits Rachel and Nic, whose story is deeply interwoven with our own. Rachel was one of Natalie Marie Jewellery’s very first bespoke clients. Entrusting Natalie to rework diamonds from a family heirloom into a truly one-of-a-kind ring Rachel would wear for life. Created during Natalie’s earliest days of bespoke, the ring has lived through marriage, children, growth, and change. Carrying layers of meaning with every chapter.

From witnessing Natalie’s graduation presentation, to becoming her first bespoke client, and collaborating on a campaign that so beautifully aligned with the ring’s aesthetic. This piece has remained a constant companion, rooted in friendship, family, and shared history.

Because love isn’t only marked in moments. It’s lived in the everyday. In friendship, in choosing each other, and in the years that unfold in between.

settings.content_intro:
DO: BUY WHAT YOU LOVE

Natalie: Simply, a piece of vintage jewellery should be chosen because it resonates with you, and because you fall in love with it. Not only are they imbued with the histories and stories of their past owners, but vintage jewellery also provides an opportunity to embrace sustainable purchasing habits – offering once-cherished designs a second (or third) lease on life.

I have always had a love for vintage jewellery, or vintage anything really. I used to spend hours in op shops collecting all sorts of odd finds – furniture, clothes, hats, even once a vintage suitcase which I used to transport and display my first ever collection! I love the mystery and enigmatic nature of vintage pieces, the opportunity for speculation on their history and journey. I also have a deep reverence for the incredible craftsmanship many of these piece’s hold. So much tradition and heritage exists in every tiny detail, which is where my love for vintage jewellery originates from.

I encourage you to find your spark. Is it a particular stone colour or cut, the setting or design style or maybe even the piece’s provenance and era that speaks to you? Is it because it reminds you of a place, someone special or loved ones from generations past? I always like to remind clients that jewellery doesn’t have to be engraved or marked with a special stone to become solely yours – it can be anything about the piece that sparks a feeling or takes you back to a moment in time.

There’s a sense of romance and allure inherit in vintage pieces, and finding the perfect design should feel akin to falling in love: a lightning bolt of recognition; the sense that you two were destined to be connected.

settings.description:
What is something that has surprised you about marriage over the years – perhaps a misconception proven wrong, or an assumption proven right? 

How natural it feels when you are with the right person. Marriage has felt less about change and more about growing side by side through different seasons of life. We genuinely enjoy being together and make time for each other, even as life gets busier. That shared choice to stay connected has been one of the most grounding.


What is something that has surprised you about marriage over the years?

How much you truly grow together. We met in our early twenties and now we are in our late thirties, we have changed, evolved, and supported each other through so many seasons of growth. Children of course bring big change, but we have always said first us, second our children, and I really believe that has been key to creating a happy and healthy family. What has stayed the same is our true friendship, we are still best friends.


In what ways do you feel your relationship has changed or grown, and in what ways has it remained the same?

We have grown up together. Life looks very different now, but the foundation has always been the same. We support each other, we are proud of each other, and we genuinely enjoy being together. That deep friendship has never changed.

settings.description:
Can you tell us a little about your connection to your ring now, years past first putting it on?

I never take it off, thanks Natalie for finally helping clean it ten years later. This ring is incredibly special in so many ways. The diamonds came from a family heirloom, and Natalie and Nic worked together to create a truly one of a kind bespoke piece that I will wear for life. It was also so special to have Natalie create it. I have had the privilege of watching her evolve her craft, from her final graduation presentation, to being one of her first bespoke clients, and later working together on a campaign that aligned so beautifully with the ring’s aesthetic. I get asked about it all the time, it holds so many stories.


Looking back over the years, could you share specific life events or experiences you've encountered with your NMJ piece? How has it become a symbol of your personal journey?

It has been there through so many chapters, marriage, children, growth, change, and everything in between. It represents family, friendship, and the life we have built together. It feels deeply personal and layered with meaning.

settings.title: “Marriage has felt less about change and more about growing side by side through different seasons of life.”

settings.description:
Beyond the physical aesthetics, can you delve into the sentimental value your NMJ piece holds for you? Are there particular moments or memories attached to it that stand out?

The sentiment is everything. Knowing the diamonds come from family, having it created by a talented friend, and wearing something that marks so many stages of life makes it incredibly special. It is a constant reminder of connection, history, and love.


If you could tell yourself one thing, or offer your past self a piece of advice back when you first met, what would that be?

Honestly, I do not think I would change a thing. We often say how lucky we are and how grateful we feel for our journey. We have always supported each other and stayed proud of the life we have created together.


What makes you most proud when it comes to your relationship?

We are coming up to eleven years this year, and we often say how proud we are of us. We balance life well together, we show up for each other, and we continue to grow side by side. Putting our relationship first has had the most beautiful knock on effect on everything that follows.

settings.title: “Putting our relationship first has had the most beautiful knock-on effect on everything that follows.”

settings.description:
Hidden within both of your wedding bands is a set of hidden birthstones, tell us what this special addition symbolises and how you chose your wedding rings.

Isaac: The inclusion of the birthstones really symbolised an extra detail to denote the bond between us, on top of everything else.

Tully: The Natalie Marie Jewellery team really helped me to articulate the vision I had for such an important forever piece. I wanted something personal with a sense of timeless permanency to it. The band is both elegant and handsome to the viewer, yet encloses a precious secret that is Isaac’s birth-stone against my skin and bone.

 

What do you love most about your life together today?

Isaac: Having just celebrated our first wedding anniversary and almost six years together it never ceases to amaze me how fun the smallest moments of an ordinary day is with Tully. Simple things like welcoming each other home from work, or walking to the markets together on the weekend. The older I get it is these moments I increasingly hold close to my heart.

Tully: To be quite honest, “Isaac” and “life” are almost synonyms to me. As W.H Auden put it, Isaac is “my working week and my Sunday rest,” and I fell in love with life in falling in love with Isaac.


At NMJ, at the heart of everything we do is love. Tough question, but tell us, what do you know about love?

Isaac: From the short time we have spent together I know that love is constantly promising to work on all aspects of our relationship together. It is that promise to be there for tomorrow. As romantic as love is, I also appreciate the soberness that love can also be. The calm assured resolve to be there together.

Tully: Love is hard work, but love is the meaning of it all.
